author: Kevin Indig
title: The Great Decoupling
content: Co-authored by Kevin Indig and Amanda Johnson, this article reveals that traffic and pipeline no longer move together. The main thesis: SEO died as a traffic channel the moment pipeline stopped following pageviews. Traffic is either down for many sites or its growth nowhere near reflects growth rates of 2019-2022, but demos and pipeline are up for brands that shifted from chasing clicks to building authority. Key issues explained: Content explosion means significantly more content competes for the same queries than 3 years ago (AI-generated articles, Reddit threads, YouTube videos, newsletters). Changing products mean the channel is structurally shifting—the products people use to find answers have fundamentally changed. Recommended strategic shifts include moving from traffic reporting to influence reporting and starting with ICP research, positioning, and omnichannel distribution (SEO + Reddit + YouTube + earned media). Stop measuring sessions, impressions, and rankings. Instead, report on brand lift, pipeline influence, and LLM visibility rates. [Note: Full article is paywalled on Substack. Content extracted from publicly available summaries and search results.]
